GlobalPolicy.wave-level-focus: Run touched module tests after every validated implementation wave.
GlobalPolicy.phase-level-focus: Run make lint, relevant integration tests, and full unit regression before phase completion.
GlobalPolicy.redaction: Never log credentials, session identifiers, prompts, user content, or provider authentication payloads.

VF-LLM-SAFE-FAILOVER.NAME: Safe same-operation failover
VF-LLM-SAFE-FAILOVER.scenario: A confirmed Claude subscription limit occurs before any side effect.
VF-LLM-SAFE-FAILOVER.expected-outcome: The same operation completes once through the mapped Codex model.

required-signals.trace-sequence: Claude limit -> safe evidence -> codex -> one consumer result

VF-LLM-CIRCUIT-RECOVERY.NAME: Automatic Claude recovery
VF-LLM-CIRCUIT-RECOVERY.scenario: The codex interval expires while concurrent requests arrive.
VF-LLM-CIRCUIT-RECOVERY.expected-outcome: Exactly one Claude probe runs; other requests continue through Codex.

required-signals.trace-sequence: codex -> one probe -> claude or bounded codex retry

VF-LLM-REPLAY-GUARD.NAME: Unsafe replay prevention
VF-LLM-REPLAY-GUARD.scenario: Claude reaches its limit after mutating, unknown, or delivered evidence.
VF-LLM-REPLAY-GUARD.expected-outcome: No Codex process starts for that operation and existing partial-failure handling remains active.

required-signals.trace-sequence: mutation evidence -> replay blocked -> zero fallback attempts

VF-LLM-READINESS.NAME: Subscription fallback readiness
VF-LLM-READINESS.scenario: Service startup and deployment verify the Codex fallback.
VF-LLM-READINESS.expected-outcome: Startup never consumes quota; deployment smoke verifies both models and capability profiles.

required-signals.trace-sequence: binary -> version -> login -> non-blocking readiness; deployment model smoke

scenarios.scenario-1: Claude success stays in claude and never starts Codex.
scenarios.scenario-2: Only typed subscription limits transition claude to codex.
scenarios.scenario-3: Concurrent requests observe one state and at most one probe.
scenarios.scenario-4: Mutation, delivery, and unknown evidence block replay.
scenarios.scenario-5: Provider selection remains below the approved 100 ms p95 budget.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: A blocked replay records zero fallback calls.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: One Telegram operation yields at most one successful provider result.

V-M-LLM-FAILOVER.wave-follow-up: Run Stage-0, WIKI runner, schema generation, and cron consumer failover tests.
V-M-LLM-FAILOVER.phase-follow-up: Run all unit tests, make lint, and the fake-CLI integration matrix.
V-M-LLM-FAILOVER.evidence: Deterministic fake clocks and async barriers prove state transitions, cooldown, reset parsing, single-flight probe, replay safety, and overhead.

scenarios.scenario-1: Every argv pins model, reasoning, sandbox, neutral cwd, ephemeral mode, ignored config, and ignored rules.
scenarios.scenario-2: gpt-5.4-mini output conforms to JSON Schema and existing Pydantic validation.
scenarios.scenario-3: gpt-5.5 medium JSONL normalizes text, usage, commands, file changes, and failures.
scenarios.scenario-4: Read-only cannot write; workspace-write reaches only the selected WIKI.
scenarios.scenario-5: Binary, exact version, login, and non-interactive checks never invoke a model.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: No API key, auth payload, prompt, user content, or provider session identifier enters logs.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Structured and text modes emit no mutation evidence.

V-M-LLM-CODEX.wave-follow-up: Run classifier and WIKI adapter compatibility tests against the fake Codex CLI.
V-M-LLM-CODEX.phase-follow-up: Run deployment smoke for both subscription models and all capability profiles.
V-M-LLM-CODEX.evidence: Unit and executable fake-CLI subprocess fixtures validate exact argv, environment allowlist, ChatGPT-only auth readiness, output parsing, cancellation, timeout, provider-local event normalization, and selected-WIKI-only write containment.

scenarios.scenario-1: Claude schema generation remains unchanged while healthy.
scenarios.scenario-2: A typed pre-write limit uses Codex text mode and validates before mutation.
scenarios.scenario-3: Dual failure preserves the existing default schema.

V-M-WIKI-MIGRATION.wave-follow-up: Run M-LLM-FAILOVER and M-LLM-CODEX module checks.
V-M-WIKI-MIGRATION.phase-follow-up: Run the WIKI unit suite and full lint gate.
V-M-WIKI-MIGRATION.evidence: Generation remains side-effect free until validate_schema succeeds and apply_generated_schema writes once.

V-M-FOUNDATION-SETTINGS.evidence: Settings remain frozen with AISW_ prefix. Tests cover Codex enablement, positive cooldown, binary, pinned version, CODEX_HOME, model and reasoning overrides, and absence of an OpenAI API-key field.

commands.cmd: uv run pytest tests/unit/test_logging_setup.py tests/unit/test_logging_traced.py tests/unit/test_logging_events_catalog.py tests/unit/test_logging_events_chunk2.py -q

V-M-FOUNDATION-LOGGING.evidence: structlog JSON renderer, ISO UTC timestamps, correlation_id contextvar bound/reset (chunk-1). @traced decorator emits <prefix>.{start,done,error} with duration_ms; never inspects args/kwargs/return (PII-safe). logging_events.py SSoT catalog of snake_case dotted event keys covers chunk-1 entrypoints and chunk-2 anchors (scheduler.job.*, storage.slow_query, claude_cli.*). CorrelationMiddleware binds correlation_id + telegram_id + chat_id + update_id into structlog.contextvars for one Update lifetime; autouse contextvars cleanup fixture between tests. PII redactor passes through chunk-2 metadata fields unchanged while still redacting free-text fields on the same record (idempotent).

V-M-AUTH-USERS.evidence: users.toml parsed/validated; allowlist atomic swap; SIGHUP+watchdog 500ms debounce; sha256 short-circuit.

V-M-STORAGE.evidence: WAL, synchronous=NORMAL, foreign_keys=ON, busy_timeout=5000 applied per connection. Chunk-2 slow-query listener attached per AsyncEngine in build_engine() of jobs/audit/sessions: emits storage.slow_query (WARNING) when one cursor execute exceeds settings.storage_slow_query_threshold_ms (default 200). Fields: db_name, statement_sha8 (sha256[:8] of raw SQL string), duration_ms. NEVER logs raw SQL or parameter values.

V-M-STORAGE-JOBS.evidence: jobs/tracker_answers/jobs_dlq schema via alembic baseline; JobPayload discriminated union round-trips (parse_job_payload); PRAGMAs verified on engine. aisw-269 (Phase-D.b.2b): DigestPayload.wiki_scope widened Literal['all'] → 'all' | list[str] (non-empty list) — DigestPayload(wiki_scope='all') and DigestPayload(wiki_scope=['health']) both validate; wiki_scope=[] rejected; model_dump(mode='json') ↔ parse_job_payload round-trips the list shape; an existing {'kind':'digest','wiki_scope':'all',...} dict still validates (no jobs.db migration; wiki_scope is not the union discriminator). VERSION 0.0.4 → 0.0.5.

V-M-STORAGE-AUDIT.evidence: 10 tables present per D-006 amendment (alembic baseline); PRAGMAs verified; append-only invariants. chat_log (D-033, aisw-kml): redact_chat_text drops the denylist (sk-ant-/Bearer/password=) before write; ChatLogWriter.write_in/write_out persist one row per turn; read_recent_window returns the last-20 / 24h ChatTurns oldest-first for the router/Stage-0 buffer.

V-M-STORAGE-SESSIONS.evidence: users/pending_users/pending_confirms/inbox_hint_cache/fsm/user_digest_prefs/user_active_wiki tables (alembic baseline + alembic/sessions/0002 + 0004 stepwise; test_baselines: stamp 0003_cards_enabled → `upgrade head` creates user_active_wiki); PRAGMAs verified. user_active_wiki (aisw-0ym): ActiveWikiPointer.set_active upserts the per-telegram_id row; get_active returns the name only within ACTIVE_WIKI_TTL_HOURS (24h), else None (stale ⇒ no force-route). user_digest_prefs (aisw-pv8): get_digest_prefs → DigestPrefs(True,True) when no users row or no prefs row; set_digest_section upserts one bool (creates the row with defaults if absent, bumps updated_at_utc), no-op + defaults for an unknown telegram_id, ValueError on an unknown section; deleting the users row CASCADE-removes the prefs row; TOGGLEABLE_DIGEST_SECTIONS ⊆ EXPAND_SECTION_KEYS and SECTION_DISPLAY_NAME keys == TOGGLEABLE_DIGEST_SECTIONS. test_baselines: stamp 0001_sessions_baseline then `alembic upgrade head` → user_digest_prefs present (0002's idempotent Base.metadata.create_all delta), and `upgrade head` from empty also creates it (baseline create_all).

commands.cmd: uv run pytest tests/unit/test_runtime_wiring.py tests/unit/test_router_adapter.py tests/unit/test_librarian_adapter.py tests/unit/test_main_hint_catalog.py tests/unit/test_main_runner_adapter.py tests/unit/test_main_digest_adapter.py -q
uv run python -m ai_steward_wiki # manual smoke with AISW_TG_BOT_TOKEN_LOCAL

V-M-RUNTIME-WIRING.evidence: Unit tests cover sync-URL derivation, data-dir creation, users.toml load fallbacks, signal handler wiring, _amain composition with mocks, missing-token fail-fast, main → asyncio.run. aisw-o6m (ADR-034): test_main_runner_adapter.py — a confident WIKI_QUERY match runs wiki_path/wiki_id scoped to the matched WIKI with no layouts block in the overlay (CLAUDE.md arrives via assemble_prompt); an ambiguous match runs cross-root at the user root with "Карта WIKI пользователя" + every WIKI's Data-layout excerpt in the overlay; a hint_catalog_resolver raising degrades to cross-root with the legacy placeholder overlay (no exception propagates); WEB_TASK never calls either resolver and keeps legacy behaviour; an adapter constructed without the two resolvers (back-compat) also keeps legacy cross-root behaviour. test_main_digest_adapter.py — a full-digest call (section=None) with extra_add_dirs appends the layouts block (header + one "## <stem>" section per extra WIKI) to planner_context before user_input; a section-expand call (/expand) with the same extra_add_dirs never injects layouts. aisw-5sd: make_hint_catalog_resolver — telegram_id → {wiki_stem: hint_text} via surrogate_id_of + owner_wikis_resolver + get_or_refresh_hint per domain CLAUDE.md; unknown sender (surrogate None) → {} (fast-path falls through); a domain with no '## Inbox hint' → omitted; no domain WIKIs → {} without touching the cache (get_or_refresh_hint monkeypatched in the unit). aisw-hcl: VoiceHandler/PhotoIngestor wired into DefaultPipeline; media _staging sweep job registered; _WikiRunnerAdapter.run promotes staged media into <wiki>/raw/media after a successful run. aisw-dsg: _RouterAdapter — ensure_inbox_wiki, _render_raw_sidecar, run_wiki_session in Inbox-WIKI/ with prompts/inbox.md, parse_router_reply, WikiRunnerError→RouterError, inbox.router.* markers. aisw-zd9: _LibrarianAdapter — resolve_target_wiki (create new dir; ROUTE-missing → create + warning; cap → rejected; bad name → rejected), stage_raw_into_wiki (raw/<ts>_text.md + promote media), run_wiki_session with wiki_id=f"{tid}/<Name>" + overlay=domain-default.md + timeout_s=None, IngestOutcome(ok|run_failed|rejected), WikiRunnerError→run_failed keeps the raw, inbox.route.* markers via capsys. Manual smoke: `python -m ai_steward_wiki` starts, logs runtime.media_pipeline.wired + polling.start, Ctrl-C → shutdown.done, exit 0.

commands.cmd: uv run pytest tests/unit/tg/test_handlers.py tests/unit/tg/test_commands.py tests/unit/tg/test_digest_sections.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_document.py -q

V-M-TG-HANDLERS-WIRING.evidence: Unit tests cover callback parsing (8 variants), router/dispatcher wiring (7 message handlers: text/voice/photo/audio/video_note/document/callback), _download_bytes, text/voice/photo/audio/video_note/document/confirm dispatch with fake aiogram Message+CallbackQuery, L1 dedup short-circuit, photo→runner(media_paths)→deliver, photo+caption, photo L2 dedup, image-document routed, STT-unavailable graceful path, fallback acks when voice/photo handlers absent. aisw-hcl (media handling): F.audio + F.video_note route to on_voice; photo handler forwards message.caption; on_photo runs Claude vision via the wiki pipeline. aisw-269 (Phase-D.b.2b — the bot's first slash commands): a dedicated Command('digest_now')/Command('expand') router registered BEFORE the F.text catch-all — a non-command text message still reaches MessagePipeline.on_text unchanged (router-order regression test). /digest_now: select the calling owner's jobs.Job rows kind=='digest_job' status=='scheduled' → for each call scheduler.firing.fire_digest_job(job_id); 0 rows → ru "no digest configured" hint (tg.command.digest_now.empty); a per-job exception is caught and logged (tg.command.digest_now.job_failed) so the remaining jobs still run; tg.command.digest_now.done with n_jobs. /expand <section>: arg ∈ {today, meds, trackers, wiki} else ru usage line (tg.command.expand.bad_section); resolve the owner WIKI set (Inbox excluded) — empty → ru notice; else digest_runner(wiki_id=primary, …, section=<key>) → reply via send_message (no run_outputs row); empty model text → ru "nothing for this section" line; tg.command.expand.delivered with section/chars; any exception at the handler boundary → generic ru error + tg.command.expand.failed. Both handlers wrap their body in try/except so nothing bubbles to the aiogram dispatcher. aisw-pv8 (Phase-D.b.2c — /digest_sections): Command('digest_sections') registered in the same commands group → firing.get_owner_digest_prefs(owner) → reply ru header + an inline keyboard (one button per TOGGLEABLE_DIGEST_SECTIONS key: text "<ru label>: вкл ✅"/"… выкл ⬜", callback_data "digestsec:<key>:<0|1>" carrying the TARGET state) + tg.command.digest_sections.shown; DigestNotInitialisedError → ru unavailable line. digestsec: callback (@router.callback_query F.data startswith "digestsec:"): parse_digestsec_callback("digestsec:<section>:<0|1>") → (section, target) — None on any malformed/foreign-prefix/unknown-section/bad-flag/wrong-arity data → cb.answer + tg.command.digest_sections.bad_callback, no DB write; valid → firing.set_owner_digest_section(owner, section=, enabled=target) → cb.message.edit_reply_markup with the rebuilt keyboard (the edit is wrapped — TelegramBadRequest "not modified" on a stale tap is swallowed, tg.command.digest_sections.edit_skipped — the DB write already succeeded) → cb.answer + tg.command.digest_sections.toggled{owner_telegram_id, section, enabled}; re-tapping a button whose state already matches is idempotent. parse_digestsec_callback unit-tested over ok + reject variants; handler-level tests use the existing aiogram test harness (firing accessors monkeypatched), mirroring test_commands.py. The new command is in the same commands-router group registered before F.text, so a plain text message still reaches MessagePipeline.on_text (router-order regression still holds).

commands.cmd: uv run pytest tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_classifier_wiring.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_router.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_hint_fastpath.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_route_ingest.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_route_confirm.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_confirm_callback.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_chat_log.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_active_wiki.py tests/unit/wiki/test_runner_aggregate.py -q

V-M-TG-PIPELINE-CLASSIFIER.evidence: Unit tests cover text happy path, L1 dup short-circuit, L2 dedup hit, classifier error → ACK_CLASSIFY_ERR_RU, runner error → ACK_RUNNER_ERR_RU, empty runner output → ACK_TEXT_RU via deliver, back-compat None injection, voice happy/empty/back-compat, structlog markers via capsys; aggregate_text across stream-json payload shapes. aisw-dsg: routable intents with a wired Router go through router.route(); router=None / non-routable use the legacy path; RouterError → ACK_RUNNER_ERR_RU; tg.pipeline.router.* markers. aisw-zd9: the ROUTE/CREATE_WIKI librarian.ingest() path (now reached via the confirm callback). aisw-e45 (Phase-C): a ROUTE/CREATE_WIKI decision (wired librarian + output) → no immediate ingest; ConfirmationService.request_explicit called with category="route_ingest" + build_route_confirm_keyboard + a draft round-trippable via route_action_from_payload; recap mentions target + notes; CLARIFY/REJECT and librarian=None → send_message(decision.notes), no confirm row. on_confirm_callback: route_ingest row + resolve→"confirmed" → ROUTE_CONFIRM_ACK_RU then librarian.ingest() with the reconstructed decision + media Paths → status="ok" → output.deliver(reply) else send_message(reply); resolve→"cancelled" → ROUTE_CONFIRM_CANCELLED_RU, no ingest; resolve→None → ROUTE_CONFIRM_STALE_RU, no ingest; non-route_ingest or missing row → legacy resolve path (tg.pipeline.confirm); tg.pipeline.route.confirm_* + confirm.route_dispatched markers via capsys. Integration (RUN_INTEGRATION=1): test_e2e_pipeline.py scenario 6 — routable text → recap + route_ingest pending row (no domain WIKI yet) → on_confirm_callback("confirm") → real Stage-1b librarian writes into the target <Domain>-WIKI/raw/, ROUTE_CONFIRM_ACK_RU sent, reply via output.deliver; tolerant of CLARIFY/REJECT. aisw-269 (Phase-D.b.2b — named-subset WIKI in the digest fast-path): after parse_recurrence, _extract_wiki_names(turn_text, owner_wiki_stems) — whole-token, case-insensitive intersection with the owner's *-WIKI/ dir-stems; all mentioned names resolve → list[str], none mentioned → 'all', a name-shaped token that does not resolve → return None → ru clarification (lists the owner's WIKI names), NO confirm draft built, NO job created (tg.pipeline.digest.wiki_unknown); the category='digest' confirm draft carries wiki_scope (the new shape) and the recap/ack names the chosen WIKIs when scoped; on confirm → create_digest_job(... wiki_scope=...). Unit: «сводка по Health каждый день в 9» with the owner having Health-WIKI/ → draft.wiki_scope==['Health'], recap names it; «сводка по Money …» with no Money-WIKI/ → clarification, no draft; no names mentioned → wiki_scope=='all' (today's behaviour). aisw-5sd (Phase-E.b — '## Inbox hint' router-bypass): a new pre-router block (between the reminder fast-path and the routable branch) guarded on intent ∈ _ROUTABLE_INTENTS ∧ router ∧ hint_catalog_resolver ∧ librarian ∧ output — builds the sender's hint catalog (hint_catalog_resolver), score_catalog(text, catalog); on is_confident → RouterDecision(intent=ROUTE, target_wiki=<stem>, raw="", parsed_ok=True), WITHOUT awaiting router.route; not confident → tg.pipeline.hint_fastpath.miss(reason=ambiguous if top_score≥MIN_SCORE else no_match) + fallthrough → heavy router runs; empty catalog → miss(reason=empty_catalog) + fallthrough, scorer not consulted; hint_catalog_resolver=None or librarian=None ⇒ block skipped, heavy router runs. aisw-2ra (silent auto-route): a confident match now routes SILENTLY (D-023 'auto') — _ingest_and_deliver(decision) runs the Stage-1b librarian.ingest immediately (no Confirm/Cancel keyboard); on status=="ok" the active-WIKI pointer is set, the reply delivered, then either ConfirmationService.request_explicit(category="route_ingest", build_route_redirect_keyboard) posts a "✅ Записал в <WIKI>" ack with a one-tap redirect picker of the owner's OTHER WIKIs (tapping reuses on_wikipick_callback) OR — when there is no other WIKI — ConfirmationService.auto_ack(ROUTE_SILENT_ACK_NOREDIR_RU); a non-ok ingest sends the composed reply via send_message and offers no redirect. _ingest_and_deliver is the single Stage-1b ingest+deliver tail shared with _handle_route_confirm. tg.pipeline.hint_fastpath.silent_route marker carries target_wiki/score/margin/status/run_id. Unit (test_pipeline_hint_fastpath.py, capsys for markers): confident hit routes silently with a redirect picker (target WIKI excluded), no-other-WIKI → plain auto_ack, failed ingest → error send + no redirect; ambiguous/no_match/empty/disabled all fall through to router.route; hint_fastpath.{catalog,silent_route,miss,fallthrough} markers asserted on their paths. Loader label (FR-2) deferred to aisw-05k.

V-M-TG-PIPELINE-STREAMING.evidence: 7 unit tests cover fast path (no placeholder, single deliver), slow path (placeholder + buffered+live feeds + final aggregate), runner exception (finalize editor + ACK_RUNNER_ERR_RU via sender), back-compat None injection, aggregate_text-as-truth, outcome.text fallback, empty-outcome ACK_TEXT_RU fallback. 3 unit tests cover run_wiki_session on_event callback (per-event delivery, exception swallow+log, no-callback back-compat). Full suite 385/385 green; make lint and grace lint clean.

V-M-CLAUDE-CLI-COMMON.evidence: resolve_binary (/-path short-circuit + shutil.which), build_env (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR + minimal PATH only), neutral_cwd (no project CLAUDE.md auto-discovery), system_prompt_argv (inlines prompt file content), truncate_stderr (UTF-8 decode + length cap), and structured Claude HTTP 429 recognition with optional ISO 8601 reset time.

commands.cmd: uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_onboarding.py tests/unit/auth/test_admin.py tests/unit/auth/test_lint_onboarding.py -q

V-M-ONBOARD-ADMIN.evidence: pending_users idempotent upsert + TTL purge; intro template slug validation (REQUIRED_SLUGS, lint_onboarding); AdminService approve/reject/elevate/demote with admin gating (NotAnAdmin) + admin_events trail (D-028); ShadowEmitter default LoggingShadowEmitter.

V-M-SCHEDULER.evidence: build_scheduler (AsyncIOScheduler + SQLAlchemyJobStore, D-003); PriorityJobQueue 5 lanes FIFO tiebreak; WikiLockManager 3-tier acquire/release + stale-PID recovery; classify_exception taxonomy + FailureCounter 3-strikes auto-disable (D-019); move_to_dlq writes jobs_dlq; kill_with_sequence SIGTERM→grace→SIGKILL (D-021); register_all_retention_jobs wires purge-pending + media _staging sweep + retention.

commands.cmd: uv run pytest tests/unit/scheduler/test_firing.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_reminder.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_digest.py tests/unit/tg/test_digest_e2e.py -q

V-M-SCHEDULER-FIRING.evidence: Pipeline reminder fast-path (BEFORE the Stage-1a router): intent==REMINDER & confidence≥THRESHOLD + future time → ConfirmationService.request_explicit with category='reminder' draft (when_utc, message, lead_time_min) + 2-button keyboard, no Job row yet; message resolution = distilled_payload['reminder_text'] if non-empty str else raw text; prefer_future=True rolls a bare past wall-clock time forward; explicitly-past absolute date → ru rejection, no confirm; escalate → ru clarification, no confirm; recurring-digest phrasing → ru "not yet", no confirm. on_confirm_callback category=='reminder' + resolve→'confirmed' → create_reminder_job writes a jobs.Job(kind='reminder_job', payload round-trips through parse_job_payload, scheduled_at_utc==when_utc) + scheduler.add_job once with a DateTrigger at when_utc, id=='reminder:<id>', misfire_grace_time is None; ack sent. cancel→ru cancelled, no Job row; resolve→None→ru stale; double-confirm idempotent. firing.fire_job: context set + Job pending → sender.send_message(chat_id, '🔔 Напоминание: <message>') + row done; Job not pending → skipped, no send; send raises → row failed + last_error + scheduler.reminder.deliver_failed; context not set → RuntimeError. ReminderPayload in parse_job_payload: valid → ReminderPayload, extra key → ValidationError. Integration: reminder message → request_explicit → simulate confirm → jobs.jobs row with kind='reminder_job' & matching scheduled_at_utc → invoke fire_job(job_id) with a fake sender → one send_message + row done. Known limitation (documented, no MVP reconciliation): a crash between the Job commit and scheduler.add_job leaves a pending row with no trigger → silent miss. (aisw-kcz; D-002/D-010/D-022; tech-spec §3/§6; ADR-006.) Digest path (aisw-oqq, Phase-D.b.1): pipeline digest fast-path (replaces the reminder branch's recurring-keyword stub): recurring-digest phrasing → parse_recurrence; on escalate → ru clarification, no confirm; else → ConfirmationService.request_explicit with category='digest' draft (recurrence serialised, wiki_scope='all', window_hours=24) + 2-button keyboard, no Job row yet. on_confirm_callback category=='digest' + resolve→'confirmed' → create_digest_job writes a jobs.Job(kind='digest_job', status='scheduled', priority==Lane.DIGEST, payload round-trips through parse_job_payload) + scheduler.add_job once with a CronTrigger (kwargs == recurrence.to_cron(), timezone == recurrence.tz), id=='digest:<id>', replace_existing=True; ack sent. cancel→ru cancelled, no Job row; resolve→None→ru stale; double-confirm idempotent. firing.fire_digest_job: context set + Job 'scheduled' → resolve owner WIKI set (minus Inbox-WIKI) → run_wiki_session called once with extra_add_dirs == the non-primary WIKI paths, under the Semaphore + WikiLockManager(primary), timeout_s==600 → assistant text (or the ru empty-digest line) delivered via tg/output (send_message or send_document); row stays status='scheduled', retry_count==0, finished_at_utc set. Job not 'scheduled' → skipped, no run. Owner has no *-WIKI → friendly ru line, finished, no strike. run_wiki_session raises (WikiRunnerError / WikiRunnerTimeoutError) → retry_count++; third consecutive failure → status='disabled' + scheduler.remove_job('digest:<id>') called + jobs_dlq row written; scheduler.digest.failed logged with error_class/retry_count/disabled. Bad/parse-failing payload → status='disabled' + DLQ immediately. context not set → DigestNotInitialisedError. DigestPayload in parse_job_payload: valid (with a Recurrence) → DigestPayload, extra key → ValidationError. run_wiki_session: extra_add_dirs paths appear in argv immediately after the primary '--add-dir <wiki_path>'; None → unchanged argv. e2e (in-process slow unit, mirrors the reminder e2e): recurring-digest text → request_explicit → simulate confirm → jobs.jobs row kind='digest_job' status='scheduled' + add_job with a CronTrigger → set_digest_context + fire_digest_job(job_id) with a fake runner-adapter (canned assistant events) → one delivery through tg/output → row still 'scheduled', retry_count==0. (aisw-oqq; D-002/D-019/D-024/D-025; tech-spec §3; ADR-007.) Digest interactive (aisw-269, Phase-D.b.2b): DigestRunner Protocol gains section:str|None=None — fire_digest_job still calls runner(...) with the default (full digest), byte-identical. fire_digest_job after resolving the owner WIKI set, when payload.wiki_scope is a list[str]: keep only (wid, path) whose wid (case-insensitive) ∈ the list → run_wiki_session gets the filtered set; scheduler.digest.scope_filter logged requested/kept/vanished; empty kept list → ru "all scoped WIKIs vanished" notice via send_message, finished_at_utc set, status stays 'scheduled' (NO strike — config drift, not a run failure), scheduler.digest.delivered logged empty='scope_vanished'. payload.wiki_scope=='all' → unchanged. create_digest_job accepts wiki_scope:str|list[str] and persists DigestPayload(wiki_scope=…); scheduler.digest.scheduled carries wiki_scope. Unit: fire_digest_job with wiki_scope=['health'] runs only that WIKI; vanished name → ru notice, no strike, status 'scheduled', scope_filter marker; create_digest_job(wiki_scope=['health','money']) persists a list shape round-trippable via parse_job_payload. (aisw-269; D-024; ADR-025.) Digest section toggles (aisw-pv8, Phase-D.b.2c): set_digest_context gains a sessions sessionmaker (the 7th context element). fire_digest_job, after building the planner context and before the runner call: get_digest_prefs(sessions_sm, owner_telegram_id) wrapped in try/except — any failure → scheduler.digest.prefs_read_failed + DigestPrefs(True,True) (degrade-to-all-on, never skip the digest); if prefs.disabled_keys is non-empty → planner_context += "\n\nНе включай разделы: <ru labels>." and scheduler.digest.sections_filtered{job_id, owner_telegram_id, disabled} logged; if empty → planner_context byte-identical to today (regression-guarded: no-prefs path calls the runner with the same string). runner(..., section=None) unchanged; the DigestRunner Protocol, _DigestRunnerAdapter, and run_section_expand are untouched (/expand ignores the toggle). get_owner_digest_prefs / set_owner_digest_section accessors (read the sessions sessionmaker from the digest context) → DigestNotInitialisedError when unwired; unit-tested in test_firing.py alongside the directive-injection cases (no-prefs byte-identical, trackers-off directive + sections_filtered marker, both-off lists both, prefs-read-failure degrades). (aisw-pv8; D-024; ADR-025/ADR-026.)

V-M-CLASSIFIER-STAGE0.evidence: classify orchestrates backend call + Pydantic validation + audit prompt_versions upsert + structlog; PromptCache semver+sha256 with reload(); Intent closed enum (D-009); parse_time dateparser→Haiku-fallback→escalate (D-010); ClaudeCliBackend resolves binary + JSON-mode envelope; AnthropicApiBackend optional; FakeClaudeRunner deterministic; INV-6 settings guard; ClassifierTimeoutError/SchemaError taxonomy.

V-M-CLASSIFIER-RECURRENCE.evidence: parse_recurrence over a ru corpus: «каждый день в 9 (утра)» → Recurrence(kind='daily', time_hhmm='09:00', tz=user_tz); «по будням в 19:00» → kind='weekly', weekdays==(mon..fri), time_hhmm='19:00'; «еженедельно по понедельникам и пятницам в 8» → weekly, weekdays==(mon,fri), '08:00'; ambiguous / no time-of-day / monthly / interval / raw-cron-string → RecurrenceParseResult(recurrence=None, escalate=True, reason set); optional Haiku fallback path returns a parsed Recurrence when the rule-based regex misses and a backend+prompt are wired, else escalate. Recurrence.to_cron(): daily → {'hour': HH, 'minute': MM}; weekly → {'day_of_week': comma-joined 3-letter names in calendar order, 'hour': HH, 'minute': MM}; round-trips through model_dump(mode='json') (frozen, extra forbidden). tz defaults to Settings.default_user_tz when the caller passes none. (aisw-oqq; D-002; tech-spec §3.)

commands.cmd: uv run pytest tests/unit/wiki/test_runner.py tests/unit/wiki/test_runner_aggregate.py tests/unit/wiki/test_runner_on_event.py tests/unit/wiki/test_streaming.py tests/unit/wiki/test_acquire.py -q

V-M-WIKI-RUNNER.evidence: run_wiki_session: assemble_prompt (base+overlay+per-WIKI CLAUDE.md atomic write), WikiLockAdapter acquire, spawn `claude --output-format stream-json`, parse_stream_json events, hard timeout + kill-sequence (WikiRunnerTimeoutError), on_event callback (per-event delivery, exception swallow); aggregate_text across stream-json shapes; classify_line pure mapping; AsyncioSpawner default.

V-M-WIKI-LIFECYCLE.evidence: normalize_wiki_name Cyrillic→Latin ISO 9 + PascalCase + slug (D-008); WikiLifecycleManager owner-scoped create/lookup/list/soft_delete/restore with AntiSpamCapError + NearDuplicateMatch (Levenshtein ≤2) + TrashRetentionExpiredError (D-041); parse/render_frontmatter + managed/user-zone markers + load_template sha256 (D-039); preflight 5-step grounding → PreflightReport.

V-M-WIKI-SCOPE.evidence: resolve_query_scope: confident single-domain match (hint_match.is_confident, MIN_SCORE/MIN_MARGIN gates) with a matching wikis dir entry → ScopeDecision(kind='scoped', target_stem, target_path, top_score, margin); ambiguous (margin below threshold), empty catalog, text over MAX_FASTPATH_CHARS, or a confidently-matched stem missing from the wikis dir mapping (race: WIKI deleted) → ScopeDecision(kind='cross', target_stem=None, target_path=None); ScopeDecision is frozen (AttributeError on mutation). collect_layouts: renders "# Карта WIKI пользователя" + one "## <stem>" section per (stem, wiki_dir) pair sourced from each WIKI's managed CLAUDE.md zone (MANAGED_START/MANAGED_END); missing CLAUDE.md, absent markers, or an empty managed body all fall back to "(схема не описана)" per WIKI; per-WIKI excerpt capped at MAX_LAYOUT_CHARS_PER_WIKI; empty wikis sequence → "". Module is pure logic — no logging of its own; wiki.run.scope / wiki.run.scope.degraded are logged at the M-RUNTIME-WIRING call sites (aisw-o6m, ADR-034).

commands.cmd: uv run pytest tests/unit/inbox/test_materialize.py tests/unit/inbox/test_parser.py tests/unit/inbox/test_hint_cache.py tests/unit/inbox/test_hint_match.py tests/unit/inbox/test_staging.py -q

V-M-INBOX.evidence: inbox_wiki_path pure <wiki_root>/<telegram_id>/Inbox-WIKI (per-user media-staging root, D-022; aisw-12t); ensure_inbox_wiki idempotent first-contact materialise (D-004); extract_inbox_hint parses "## Inbox hint" body or None (D-016); InboxHintCacheRepo UPSERT/get/invalidate + get_or_refresh_hint stat→cache-hit on (size,mtime) else read+sha256; score_catalog token-overlap count (message ∩ each domain's hint+stem-name tokens, normalised: casefold word-runs, drop <3-char + stop-words) → ranked HintMatch; is_confident iff one domain has ≥MIN_SCORE(2) matched keywords AND ≥MIN_MARGIN(1) lead over #2 — single clear winner / ambiguous (gap too small) / weak winner / empty catalog / stem-name folding / token normalisation / threshold boundaries unit-covered (aisw-5sd); stage_media atomic write to _staging/<run_id>_<sha8>.<ext>; promote_to_raw / promote_path_to_raw atomic move to raw/media/<ISO8601>_<sha8>.<ext>; sweep_staging TTL delete (one inbox_root) + sweep_all_user_staging over every per-user Inbox-WIKI (D-022).

V-M-INBOX-ROUTER.evidence: parse_router_reply: happy path for each RouterIntent (route/create_wiki/clarify/reject), multi-line notes, target_wiki null/empty → None, preamble + quoted example → last ```router block wins, missing block → RouterDecision(intent=CLARIFY, parsed_ok=False, notes=trimmed-original-or-generic), unknown intent value → fallback, ROUTE/CREATE_WIKI without target → demoted to CLARIFY, RouterDecision frozen + extra=forbid, RouterError is an Exception (aisw-dsg; prompts/inbox.md ≥1.1.0).

V-M-INBOX-ROUTE.evidence: resolve_target_wiki: CREATE_WIKI → new dir, created=True; idempotent second call → created=False; ROUTE existing → created=False; ROUTE missing → create + on_route_missing callback; AntiSpamCapError → RouteRejection("cap"); WikiNameError → RouteRejection("bad_name"). stage_raw_into_wiki: text → raw/<ts>_text.md body verbatim, media_rel=[]; voice/photo → .md sidecar with YAML front-matter + promote_path_to_raw into raw/media/ (StagedRaw.media_abs absolute & exists, source moved); already-gone media → skipped. pick_domain_overlay: domain-<slug>.md if present else domain-default.md. build_ingest_prompt references the raw path(s) + the user text (aisw-zd9; D-004). aisw-e45 (Phase-C): route_action_to_payload ∘ route_action_from_payload is identity for ROUTE and CREATE_WIKI decisions with/without media (RouterIntent/RouterDecision/Path reconstructed by type; missing media → []); from_payload raises ValueError on a missing 'decision' or a bad 'source'.

V-M-INGEST-IDEM.evidence: IdempotencyService.check_update_id (L1 tg_updates, 24h TTL), check_content (L2 seen_files, SHA-256, 30d TTL), record_dedup_choice (dedup_hits); normalize_text NFKC+strip+lower+collapse-ws for text hashing; compute_content_hash per ContentKind; SeenFileMatch on collision (D-018 amended 2026-05-10, INV-4).

V-M-OPS-PII.evidence: PIIRedactor tiered redaction (NIST SP 800-122) with HMAC secret + on/off toggles + make_structlog_processor; module-level redact convenience; purge_user GDPR endpoint admin-gated + allow-listed stores (D-034 §10.4); RETENTION_POLICIES matrix; run_purge with dry_run + cutoff_utc/oldest_kept_utc; register_retention_jobs adds §10.4 cron; purge_trash_sweep tier-1/tier-2 final sweep; purge_staging wraps inbox.staging.sweep_staging.

V-M-OPS-BACKUP.evidence: snapshot_databases VACUUM INTO state/snapshots/<UTC>/ for jobs/audit/sessions (0700 dirs); purge_old_snapshots 7d rolling retention; register_db_snapshot_job cron 03:00 UTC (D-037, INV-2/INV-3); restore test reloads a snapshot; init_wiki_git idempotent git init + .gitignore; format_commit_message "<job_id>(<category>): <title>"; auto_commit stage-all + commit, no-op when nothing staged; WikiGitError on CLI failure.

commands.cmd: uv run pytest tests/unit/tg/test_bot.py tests/unit/tg/test_middleware_auth.py tests/unit/tg/test_middleware_start_bypass.py tests/unit/tg/test_confirm.py tests/unit/tg/test_output.py tests/unit/tg/test_stream_edit.py -q

V-M-TG-TEXT.evidence: build_bot (HTML parse_mode) + build_dispatcher (AllowlistMiddleware outer); AllowlistMiddleware denies unknown telegram_id with DENY_TEXT_RU + auth.deny, /start bypass, carries user_record (D-031, D-042); ConfirmationService auto/implicit/explicit + 10min TTL + compute_payload_hash + 3-button keyboard (D-023); request_explicit accepts a keyboard_factory (default 3-button; build_route_confirm_keyboard → 2-button confirm/cancel for Inbox-WIKI route confirms, aisw-e45); deliver_output ≤3500 inline / ≤10000 ChainSplitter (i/M footer, ≤3 parts) / >10000 HaikuSummarizer→LengthCapSummarizer + RunOutput persistence (D-024, D-025); HtmlBalancer tag balancing; StreamEditor 1.5s/Δ≥50 throttle + chain-split at 4000 + final-flush on end/exception/cancel (D-026).

V-M-TG-VOICE.evidence: FasterWhisperTranscriber lazy CTranslate2 int8 CPU model, ru/en, RTF target ≤0.5, fallback lang ru, raises VoiceUnavailableError on missing faster-whisper; VoiceHandler stages bytes via inbox.staging.stage_media then transcribes → (MediaRef, Transcript) (D-022); audio + video_note route here from handlers.

V-M-TG-PHOTO.evidence: PhotoIngestor stages photo bytes to _staging/ via inbox.staging.stage_media → MediaRef; PHOTO_MIME_TO_EXT closed mapping of allowed photo MIME types; photos (and image-documents) run Claude vision via the wiki pipeline media_paths (D-022, tech-spec §9).

V-M-SCHEDULER-CRON-USER.evidence: create_cron_user_job INSERTs jobs.Job(kind='cron_user', status='scheduled', priority=Lane.CRON_WRITE, payload=CronUserPayload(recurrence:Recurrence, command:str, wiki_id:str|None)) and registers AsyncIOScheduler.add_job(fire_cron_user_job, CronTrigger(**recurrence.to_cron(), timezone=user_tz), args=[job_id], id=f'cron_user:{job_id}', replace_existing=True); commit-before-add_job; returns int job_id. fire_cron_user_job(job_id:int) loads Job, guards status=='scheduled' (vanished/wrong-status row → log scheduler.cron_user.fire.job_missing + return — idempotent on APScheduler replay), constructs CronUserQueueMsg(job_id, owner_telegram_id, chat_id, command, correlation_id=uuid4().hex, scheduled_at_utc=datetime.now(UTC)), pushes via queue.put(Lane.CRON_WRITE, msg), UPDATE status='queued'; raises CronUserContextNotInitialisedError when set_cron_user_context never called. Mirrors firing.py shape — module-level _ctx registry; picklable int callback for SQLAlchemyJobStore (aisw-02v; D-002; tech-spec §3 D-011).

V-M-SCHEDULER-CONSUMER.evidence: CronConsumer.run() drains PriorityJobQueue.get() in a single async loop (NFR-1 concurrency=1, at-most-once); per item: CronUserQueueMsg.model_validate raises ValidationError → log+continue; UPDATE jobs.Job status='running' + started_at_utc=now_utc; spawn subprocess via asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(*argv, stdout=PIPE, stderr=PIPE, cwd=neutral_cwd(claude_config_dir), env=build_env(claude_config_dir)) where argv = [resolve_binary(claude_binary), *system_prompt_argv(prompt_path), '--setting-sources','','--disable-slash-commands','--permission-mode','dontAsk','--disallowedTools','WebFetch','--', command] — the Claude CLI runs DIRECTLY, NO systemd-run wrapper (aisw-abc/ADR-010 simple single-user model; CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR via build_env, parity with M-WIKI-RUNNER); asyncio.wait_for(proc.communicate(), timeout=600); TimeoutError → kill_with_sequence(proc, grace_seconds=DEFAULT_TERM_GRACE_SECONDS) → status='failed' + reply '❌ Тайм-аут 10 минут.'; exit_code==0 → ChainSplitter().split(stdout.decode('utf-8','replace')) chunked bot.send_message * n_chunks + status='finished'; exit_code!=0 → '❌ Ошибка ({code}): '+truncate_stderr(stderr, limit=512) + status='failed'; TelegramAPIError on send → log scheduler.consumer.deliver_failed + status='failed' (no re-raise — loop survives). Cancellation: CancelledError propagates out of .run() so __main__ shutdown can await the task. CronUserQueueMsg Pydantic v2 discriminated union with discriminator='kind' (NFR-5) round-trips via model_dump_json. Runner-parity hardening (aisw-o3m): _build_argv now inserts --setting-sources "" / --disable-slash-commands / --permission-mode dontAsk / --disallowedTools WebFetch after system_prompt_argv and before the aisw-0j4 literal '--' + command separator (positional-prompt invocation model kept — no -p/stream-json switch); test_consumer.py asserts the flags are present and that the '--' guard still precedes a '-'-prefixed command, and that argv[0] is the claude binary with no systemd-run/--scope/--slice present (aisw-abc). (aisw-02v; aisw-abc; ADR-010; R-1 R-2 R-6 mitigations; D-011 §3; D-021).

V-M-TG-CRON-ADD.evidence: register_cron_add_handlers(router, get_user_tz) registers Command('cron_add'). Handler splits CommandObject.args on the first '|' (left=schedule_text trimmed, right=command trimmed). Cases: (a) no '|' OR empty schedule OR empty command → reply CRON_ADD_USAGE_RU + CRON_ADD_USAGE_RU_HINT, log tg.command.cron_add.usage; (b) parse_recurrence(schedule_text, user_tz=await get_user_tz(owner_telegram_id)) returns RecurrenceParseResult(escalate=True, reason=...) → reply CRON_ADD_USAGE_RU_HINT, log tg.command.cron_add.escalate reason=...; (c) parsed Recurrence → create_cron_user_job(...) → int job_id → reply '✅ Запланировано (id={job_id}): {_humanize_recurrence(rec)}. Команда: {command_preview}', log tg.command.cron_add.parsed + tg.command.cron_add.scheduled job_id=...; (d) defensive try/except around the create+answer wrapper → reply _GENERIC_ERR_RU + log tg.command.cron_add.failed error_class=... (handler never bubbles to the dispatcher). _humanize_recurrence pure: daily → 'каждый день в HH:MM ({tz})'; weekly → 'каждую {ru weekday list} в HH:MM ({tz})'; monthly → 'каждое {day_of_month}-е число в HH:MM ({tz})'. (aisw-02v; R-4 mitigation: parser-escalate becomes user-visible usage hint, no silent bad-cron).

Gate-Phase-G.1.goal: Provider circuit, typed limits, replay evidence, and concurrency are deterministic.

required-evidence.evidence-1: claude, codex, and probe transitions match every approved path.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Unsafe replay starts no fallback process.

Gate-Phase-G.2.goal: Codex invocation, readiness, output parsing, and capability profiles are isolated and deterministic.

required-evidence.evidence-1: Every invocation uses dedicated CODEX_HOME and explicit flags.
required-evidence.evidence-2: No readiness check invokes a model.

Gate-Phase-G.3.goal: Structured and text consumers preserve their existing public contracts.

commands.command-1: uv run pytest tests/unit/classifier tests/unit/wiki/test_schema_gen.py tests/unit/scheduler/test_consumer.py -q

required-evidence.evidence-2: Schema and cron paths perform external effects only after successful generation.

Gate-Phase-G.4.goal: WIKI failover preserves locking, transcript, streaming, cancellation, and replay safety.

required-evidence.evidence-1: One WIKI lock spans both safe attempts.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Normalized Codex events satisfy current aggregation and delivery consumers.

Gate-Phase-G.5.goal: Runtime wiring, documentation, deployment smoke, and project-wide regression are complete.

required-evidence.evidence-1: Startup remains available when Codex readiness fails.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Operator smoke verifies both configured subscription models and containment.
